body.title: Sonnet 1M context auto-promote silently bypasses /autocompact
body.result: Added per-ticket count guard (fire /autocompact every 5 tickets) to sprint-batch SKILL.md; added hard rule; updated autocompact SKILL.md with 1M note
body.intention: I intend that /autocompact fires reliably even when Sonnet uses a 1M context window.
body.description:
Sonnet automatically promotes to 1M context window, which causes /autocompact to be silently skipped (the context never hits the threshold that triggers compact). Sessions grow until they halt rather than rolling over cleanly. This is a new failure mode (observed 2026-06-05).

Needs investigation: does CC have a way to detect or limit the context window being used? Options: (1) enforce /autocompact after N turns regardless of context size, (2) log a warning when context > threshold, (3) add a turn counter to sprint-ticket that triggers /autocompact after K tickets.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/sprint-batch/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/skills/autocompact/SKILL.md
**Design rules:** /autocompact is the block-end signal; must fire reliably
**Scope boundary:** In scope: detect or work around 1M context auto-promote. Out of scope: changing Claude API behavior.
**Completion criteria:** sprint-batch fires /autocompact after each N tickets regardless of context window size; or equivalent guard documented and tested.
